<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nikken]][[ja:日乾]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nikken]][[ja:日乾]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日乾]]
{{Infobox person|image=File:Nose Myokenzan Nikken.jpg|birth_date=1560|death_date={{death date|1635|12|6}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|WD=Q205662}}|religion={{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|WD=Q871371|1=Nichiren Buddhism}}}}
{{Unreferenceds improvement|date=December 21, 2022, 10:46 (UTC)}}

[[File:Nose Myokenzan Nikken.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Statue of Nikken at Nose Myokenzan]]

'''Nikken''' (日乾, 1560 - December 6, 1635) was a Buddhist monk of the {{ill|Nichiren-shū|en|Nichiren-shū|WD=Q206611|lt=Nichiren-shū|1=Nichiren-shū}} sect active during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} to the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. He was born in {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}} and had the Dharma name "Kōjun" (孝順). He also bore the title "Jakushō-in" (寂照院). Nikken gained the patronage of {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu|1=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, the lord of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}'s {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain|1=Kishū Domain}}. With Yorinobu's support, Nikken played a significant role in the revival of [[Rennyō-ji Temple]] (蓮永寺) in {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}}.
== See Also ==
* [[Jōshō-ji Temple (Kyoto)]]
* [[Sadoga Hajime Hokke Honzon]] (佐渡始顕本尊)

{{Buddhism-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|日乾|version=103167267|comment=Imported full ja history}}



[[ja:日乾]]
[[ja:日乾&]]
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{Commons category|Nikken}}
{{wikidata link|Q11504819}}

[[Category:1635 deaths]]

[[Category:Monks of the Muromachi and Azuchi–Momoyama Periods]]
[[Category:Nichiren-shū Buddhist monks]]
[[Category:People of Wakasa Province]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
[[Category:Qq]]
